cases.title: Independently switchable route
cases.description: A new information route has its own configuration switch and parent-relative ablation.
cases.subject: model-information-flow
cases.rationale: The route is an independently selectable structural dimension.

cases.title: Pooling mode inside one representation mechanism
cases.description: Mean versus another pooling rule changes a parameter of an already admitted representation mechanism.
cases.subject: pooling-mode
cases.rationale: An ablatable parameter choice does not automatically create a second Feature for the same mechanism.

cases.title: Codebook size inside one VQ mechanism
cases.description: Changing codebook count or size remains a parameter of the product-VQ Feature.
cases.subject: codebook-count-and-size
cases.rationale: The values belong in design, delta and experiments rather than separate tree nodes.

cases.title: Cumsum for module-internal self-DD state
cases.description: A recurrent cumsum replaces module-internal layer-history state and can be switched independently.
cases.subject: module-internal-self-dd
cases.rationale: The changed state belongs to the module-internal connection mechanism.

cases.title: Cumsum for cross-module route sources
cases.description: The same cumsum technique changes a separately switchable cross-module source object.
cases.subject: cross-module-route-source
cases.rationale: A different independently controlled subject permits a second Feature despite technique reuse.

cases.title: Semantically equivalent TransformerBlock replacement
cases.description: A custom Python class is replaced by a standard TransformerBlock without changing model semantics.
cases.subject: hlm-layer-implementation
cases.rationale: Changing Python classes alone is a refactor when numerical and structural semantics are equivalent.

cases.title: TransformerBlock replacement with unresolved equivalence
cases.description: Normalization, activation or attention details may differ, but equivalence has not yet been established.
cases.subject: hlm-layer-implementation
cases.rationale: The proposal is retained only while a possible structural difference remains unresolved.
cases.downgrade_condition: If algorithm or numerical-parity review confirms semantic equivalence, downgrade this proposal to implementation evidence.

cases.title: Representation mechanism found in an initial snapshot
cases.description: One independently ablatable representation mechanism is first observable in an aggregated snapshot.
cases.subject: representation-stream
cases.rationale: Shared commit provenance does not erase an independently reviewable mechanism boundary.

cases.title: Routing mechanism found in the same initial snapshot
cases.description: A separately switchable routing mechanism shares the snapshot commit with the representation mechanism.
cases.subject: cross-module-information-route
cases.rationale: The common initial revision is evidence provenance, not a forced merge rule.
